sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports difficulty swallowing over a period of time, with pain when swallowing certain foods. He notes that the difficulty is exacerbated by larger food pieces. He has experienced some weight gain over the past month and reports feeling stressed due to a recent move. He also mentions a stressful period in his life, leading to increased consumption of pizza and burgers. He reports his dog's travel is a source of stress and worries about the dog's well-being during air travel, specifically regarding cargo hold issues.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 130/77 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm. Oxygen saturation is 100%. Respiratory rate is 19 breaths per minute. Temperature is 98.6°F. Physical exam reveals no rash, lesions, or tenderness to palpation in the neck. Abdomen is nondistended with active bowel sounds. Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. Neurological exam is unremarkable. An upper endoscopy is scheduled.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: Cute esophagitis is suspected based on the patient's reported symptoms and barium swallow findings. The patient's weight gain is a contributing factor to his symptoms. The stress and recent move may be exacerbating the esophageal issues.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe protonix 40mg once daily. Prescribe carafate 1g four times daily for one month. Schedule an upper endoscopy for follow-up. Encourage the patient to change their diet, decreasing alcohol and caffeine intake, especially once settled in his new location. Advise the patient to eat slowly and chew food thoroughly. Advise against citrus foods, fruits, and spicy foods until symptoms improve. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week.
